items.title: Front offices originally worked under the assumption that the NBA salary cap would rise the max 10% each season to $228 million in 2029-30.
items.body_excerpt:
Front offices originally worked under the assumption that the NBA salary cap would rise the max 10% each season to $228 million in 2029-30.

Now they’re projected to have $35m less than expected that season.

Production per dollar has become one of the most important stats in the league.

images.alt: Line chart titled "The NBA's Vanishing Cap Room," subtitled "Original salary cap projections vs. current projections." Two lines from 2024-25 to 2029-30: original projections (gray dashed) rising from $140M to $228M, and current projections (navy solid) rising from $140M to $193M. Both lines match through 2025-26 at $155M, then diverge — gaps of −$6M in '26-27, −$15M in '27-28, −$25M in '28-29, and −$35M in '29-30, shown as a shaded pink area between the lines. Data: Dunc'd On Prime. Viz: @HoopInformatics.bsky.social.

A handful of people asked why the big Nets-Hornets-Bulls-Wolves trade hasn't gone official yet...

That deal will have to wait until July 9. Mouhamadou Gueye, who the Bulls are trading as part of the deal, isn't eligible to be traded until July 9 because he was signed on April 9.
